channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Albanese government announces new AI office and proposes standards requiring large data centres to match their electricity use with power generation
item.description: The Albanese government has announced a significant shift in Australia's approach to artificial intelligence by introducing a new regulatory framework aimed at supporting the country's ambitions to be...

item.title: Enid Bennett: A Pioneering Actress of Early Hollywood
item.description: Enid Bennett (1893-1969) was an Australian-born silent film actress who became a prominent figure in early American cinema during the late 1910s and early 1920s. After arriving in the U.S. in 1915, sh...

item.title: FIFA's Brand Value Surpasses $5.2 Billion Following 2026 World Cup Success
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup, hosted jointly by the United States, Canada, and Mexico, has significantly boosted the organization's brand value, according to a Brand Finance Africa analysis. The tournament...

item.title: E Atkinson to Miss Second Test Against New Zealand Following Curfew Breach and ECB Probe into Nightclub Incident
item.description: The article details the potential exclusion of England cricketer E Atkinson from the second Test against New Zealand due to a curfew violation. The ECB has initiated an investigation into a nightclub ...

item.title: Southern California Faces Elevated Wildfire Risk Amid Extreme Heatwave
item.description: A severe heatwave is expected to peak in Southern California on Wednesday, pushing temperatures into triple-digit territory and heightening wildfire risks. Meteorologist Mike Wofford of the National W...

item.title: Canadian Forces and Police Safely Remove Historic Explosive Device from Portapique Shore
item.description: Canadian police and military authorities have successfully removed an old military ordnance that washed ashore in Portapique, Nova Scotia. The explosive device, discovered in the water near Beachcombe...

item.title: 90-Year-Old Doctor Conquers Mount Kilimanjaro, Celebrates Birthday at Summit
item.description: Dr. Art Ulene, a 90-year-old retired American physician, has become a symbol of human endurance after summiting Mount Kilimanjaro’s Uhuru Peak. His achievement, marked by a birthday celebration at 5,8...

item.title: Stella Pleads with Bea to Avoid Grudges Against Vincent in The Alibi's Episode 86
item.description: In the latest episode of 'The Alibi,' titled Episode 86, Stella Morales continues to navigate complex relationships within the show's narrative. The episode centers on Stella's heartfelt plea to her f...

item.title: England's World Cup Semi-Final Clash with Argentina: A Tactical Battle Against Messi's Mastery
item.description: The article details England's World Cup semi-final against Argentina, focusing on manager Thomas Tuchel's challenge to stop Lionel Messi and secure a historic victory. It highlights the intense rivalr...

item.title: Laura Ingraham Criticizes Report Ranking States as Worst Places to Live
item.description: Laura Ingraham, a Fox News host, expressed frustration over a CNBC report ranking predominantly Trump-victorious states among the worst places to live in 2026. She questioned the logic of the list, wh...

item.title: China's Agricultural Investments in Africa Lack Processing and Storage Support, Hindering Development
item.description: China has become one of Africa's largest development financiers, with over $180 billion in loans to African countries since 2000. While agricultural funding focuses on farm development, irrigation, an...

item.title: Tanzania's Tourism Sector Surpasses USD 4.41 Billion in 2025 with 2.29 Million Visitors
item.description: Tanzania's tourism sector recorded significant growth in 2025, with international arrivals reaching 2,294,495, a 7.1% increase from 2024. Tourism earnings rose to USD 4,410.6 million, up 13% from USD ...

item.title: AC/DC's 2026 North American Tour Setlist Features Classic Hits
item.description: AC/DC kicked off their 2026 North American tour in Charlotte, North Carolina, delivering a 21-song setlist packed with fan-favourites. The performance included iconic tracks such as 'Back In Black', '...

item.title: The Age publishes its Superquiz and Mini Crossword for Thursday, 16 July
item.description: This article is a promotional and index page for The Age's daily puzzle offerings rather than a traditional news report. It announces the availability of the Superquiz for Thursday, 16 July, written b...

item.title: India and UK Sign Comprehensive Trade Agreement to Boost Economic Ties
item.description: The India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA) has officially taken effect, significantly reducing tariffs on thousands of goods and expanding market access for services between the tw...

item.title: Wildfires in Ontario Cause Air Quality Warnings and Evacuations
item.description: Smoke from wildfires in northwestern Ontario is drifting south, leading to poor air quality in Toronto and southern Ontario. Environment Canada has issued warnings that this condition may persist unti...

item.title: Political Defections and ED Summons: TMC Factional Struggles
item.description: Madan Mitra, a former TMC minister, has switched allegiance to the rebel faction led by Ritabrata Banerjee, citing the Enforcement Directorate's (ED) summons to his family as the reason. Mahua Moitra,...

item.title: Kenya Power Conducts Scheduled Maintenance with Outages in Seven Counties
item.description: Kenya Power and Lighting Company has announced planned electricity outages across seven counties on Thursday, July 16. The maintenance work includes enhancing new connections, system upgrades, and rep...

item.title: Christian Yoka's efforts to stabilize Congo's finances
item.description: Christian Yoka, the Republic of Congo's finance minister, has taken significant steps to address the country's financial challenges. Drawing on his experience at the French Development Agency, Yoka ha...

item.title: Australian Theatre Industry Faces Struggles Amid Musical Cuts
item.description: The Australian theatre industry is experiencing significant challenges, with several globally successful musicals ending their runs prematurely. Shows like 'Waitress: The Musical' and 'Beetlejuice' (c...
